zh: 谈谈这个解锁，然后谈谈你实际运行的爪子是什么以及它们做什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: Part of I think where people stumble with OpenClaw is they read about OpenClaw is running my business, and they think they can throw any task at a single agent and get great results.
zh: 我认为人们在 OpenClaw 上跌跌撞撞的一部分原因是，他们读到 OpenClaw 正在经营我的业务，他们可以向单个智能体抛出任何任务并获得出色的结果。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then they get really frustrated.
zh: 然后他们变得非常沮丧。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know if you've had this experience, my OpenClaw sometimes forgets what we talked about yesterday, even though they have a memory, it sometimes loses access to my email and asks me to reauthenticate or says it can't access a file that it can.
zh: 我不知道你是否有这种经历，我的 OpenClaw 有时会忘记我们昨天谈论的内容，即使它们有记忆，它有时会失去对我电子邮件的访问权限并要求我重新认证，或者说它无法访问它可以访问的文件。

speaker: Unknown
en: And this really comes down to one concept that I think we're all really familiar with, which is context overload.
zh: 这真的归结为一个我认为我们都很熟悉的概念，那就是上下文过载。

speaker: Unknown
en: We've seen this with ChatGPT, if you're using Claude Code for coding, the longer you go and fill out the context window, the harder it is for the agent to do a good job at the task at hand.
zh: 我们在 ChatGPT 上见过这种情况，如果你使用 Claude Code 进行编码，随着时间推移并填满上下文窗口，智能体越难做好手头的任务。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so you can manage your context window and OpenClaw does by compressing history at the end of a conversation or starting a fresh session, but I manage context windows even more efficiently by sectioning off which tasks go to which agent.
zh: 因此你可以管理你的上下文窗口，OpenClaw 通过在对话结束时压缩历史记录或开始一个新会话来做到这一点，但我通过将哪些任务交给哪个智能体来更高效地管理上下文窗口。

speaker: Unknown
en: So even between Polly and Finn, my work assistant and my family assistant, they're both doing scheduling and calendaring and email and admin stuff, but Polly has enough to worry about with the work stuff that I don't need her thinking about the kids' soccer schedule as well.
zh: 因此即使在 Polly 和 Finn 之间，我的工作助理和家庭助理，她们都在做日程安排、日历管理、邮件和行政事务，但 Polly 有足够的工作内容要操心，我不需要她同时也考虑孩子的足球赛日程。

speaker: Unknown
en: And Finn has enough to think about that I don't need her replying to emails in my work inbox.
zh: 而 Finn 也有足够的事情要思考，我不需要她回复我工作收件箱里的邮件。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so that's where I started to really feel, oh, I would hire different people to do this job in real life, so I'm going to, quote unquote, hire different agents to do this job in my agent team.
zh: 因此也就是在那儿我开始真正感觉到，哦，在现实生活中我会雇不同的人来做这项工作，所以我将，所谓的，雇佣不同的智能体来在我的智能体团队中做这项工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then once I got that unluck, boy, did I start creating agents?
zh: 而一旦我解开了这个谜题，天哪，我开始创建智能体了吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: So now we have Polly, Finn, Max, Howie, Sam, Kelly, Holly, and Sage and Q, nine.
zh: 所以现在我们有 Polly、Finn、Max、Howie、Sam、Kelly、Holly，还有 Sage 和 Q，九个。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then my husband has one over here.
zh: 然后我的丈夫在那边有一个。

speaker: Unknown
en: He takes a very different approach to naming.
zh: 他采用了非常不同的命名方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: His is named Martron1000.
zh: 他的那个名叫 Martron1000。

speaker: Unknown
en: So naming your OpenClaw is a very fun part of this process.
zh: 所以给你的 OpenClaw 命名是这个过程中非常有趣的一部分。

speaker: Unknown
en: Some people may think this as AI psychosis, having nine OpenClaw's.
zh: 有些人可能认为这是 AI 精神病，拥有九个 OpenClaw。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not psychosis.
zh: 这不是精神病。

speaker: Unknown
en: Let me change your mind here.
zh: 让我在这里改变你的想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: Let's just make it a little bit simpler for you.
zh: 让我们为你把它简化一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't actually think that, just to be clear.
zh: 我其实不这么认为，澄清一下。

speaker: Unknown
en: I have nine Slack channels that I do my work in.
zh: 我有九个 Slack 频道我在其中工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: I wouldn't put it all in general.
zh: 我不会把它们都放在 general 里。

speaker: Unknown
en: I have nine Slack channels with my team and my marketing team's in one, and my sales team's in another, and my dev team's in another, and my development team does not care what was posted on X today, and my podcast team does not care what's in the ChatPRD sales pipeline.
zh: 我和我的团队有九个 Slack 频道，我的营销团队在一个，我的销售团队在另一个，我的开发团队在另一个，我的开发团队不在乎今天在 X 上发布了什么，我的播客团队不在乎 ChatPRD 的销售管线里有什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so we don't have to make it weird.
zh: 因此我们不必把它变得很奇怪。

speaker: Unknown
en: We can make it very practical, which is channels and different areas for different lanes of work and they intersect when they need to intersect.
zh: 我们可以把它变得很实用，也就是频道和针对不同工作领域的不同区域，它们在需要交叉的时候交叉。

speaker: Unknown
en: And otherwise we don't bother our colleagues with it.
zh: 否则我们不会用这些打扰我们的同事。

speaker: Unknown
en: That is such a good way of framing it.
zh: 这是一个非常好的框架方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I never thought of it, the limitation is context and memory and having it focused and not forget basically.
zh: 我从没想过这点，局限性在于上下文和记忆，以及让它保持专注且基本不忘事。

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, and we're all coming out of many, many years of remote working and we know how context fried we all get in something like Slack where we're watching, "Oh, this customer conversation popped up in this channel and then I got a DM over here," and imagine if that was all happening in one stream of consciousness thread and you had to process it, that would be very hard.
zh: 嗯，而且我们都刚经历了多年的远程工作，我们都知道在像 Slack 这样的地方我们会被上下文折磨得多么焦头烂额，我们在那看着，“哦，这个客户对话在这个频道里弹出来，然后我在这里收到了一条私信，”想象如果这些都发生在一个连续的意识流线程中而你必须处理它，那会非常难。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I just take that same mental model and I say, "Sam's not going to be able to process, by stream of consciousness, business and know when he needs to come in and when he doesn't.
zh: 因此我只是采用同样的心智模式并说，“Sam 将无法通过意识流处理商业事务并知道他什么时候需要介入，什么时候不需要。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I'm going to give his own quiet room and we're only going to talk about the stuff that he cares about there."
zh: 所以我要给他一个安静的房间，我们只在那里谈论他关心的东西。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Imagine your advice is start with one, play around and then add more.
zh: 我想你的建议是先从一个开始，玩一玩，然后再添加更多。

speaker: Unknown
en: Don't just jump in in all the different use cases.
zh: 不要直接跳进所有不同的用例。

speaker: Claire Vo
en: And then add more.
zh: 然后再添加更多。

speaker: Unknown
en: Cool.
zh: 酷。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then buy a bunch of MacBooks.
zh: 然后买一堆 MacBook。

speaker: Unknown
en: How do you do multiple claws?
zh: 你如何运行多个 Claws？

speaker: Unknown
en: Do you need a separate computer for each one?
zh: 你需要为每一个配备一台独立的电脑吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: You don't.
zh: 不需要。

speaker: Unknown
en: And in fact, I have a Mac mini over here.
zh: 事实上，我这里有一个 Mac mini。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's very classy.
zh: 它很有档次。

speaker: Unknown
en: It doesn't have the lobster headband.
zh: 它没有那个龙虾头带。

speaker: Unknown
en: It has a piece of painter's tape on which I've scribbled Polly and Crew.
zh: 它上面贴着一条画画用的胶带，我在上面涂写了 Polly 和 Crew。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so if you haven't watched my How I AI episode with Jesse Genet, she's the one that got my mental model really unlocked around separate machines.
zh: 所以如果你没看过我和 Jesse Genet 的《How I AI》那一集，就是她真正让我解开了关于独立机器的心智模式。

speaker: Unknown
en: And her concept and my concept now is if I'm okay with every agent on the machine occasionally going into each other's space, occasionally going into each other's tools, occasionally reading each other's docs, they can all live on the same machine.
zh: 她的概念和我现在的概念是，如果我可以接受机器上的每个智能体偶尔进入彼此的空间，偶尔使用彼此的工具，偶尔阅读彼此的文档，它们都可以生活在同一台机器上。

speaker: Unknown
en: So you know what?
zh: 所以你知道吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't necessarily want Sam the salesperson looking in GitHub, it's not his job, but it doesn't cause me any heartache as an employer if my salesperson has access to reading GitHub.
zh: 我不一定想让做销售的 Sam 看 GitHub，这不是他的工作，但如果我的销售人员有权阅读 GitHub，作为雇主这并不会让我感到心痛。

speaker: Unknown
en: Not a big deal.
zh: 没什么大不了的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so they can all live on the same machine.
zh: 因此它们都可以生活在同一台机器上。

speaker: Unknown
en: But Finn, for example, my family agent, I'm going to move to his own machine.
zh: 但是 Finn，例如，我的家庭智能体，我要把他移到他自己的机器上。

speaker: Unknown
en: He has no business knowing what's going on in my work, so I don't need him in there.
zh: 他没必要知道我工作里发生了什么，所以我不需要他在那里。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I'm going to add my personal email address, which I haven't added so far in this workspace, and he's going to live physically partitioned from the rest of that team.
zh: 我要添加我的个人邮箱地址，我目前在这个工作空间里还没添加过，他将在物理上与那个团队的其余部分隔离。

speaker: Unknown
en: Just like people walk around with their personal phone and their work phone and they say, "These are just different things that I do different work in," and keep those physically partitioned so that they literally cannot cross boundaries.
zh: 就像人们随身带着个人手机和工作手机，他们会说，“这些只是我在不同工作中使用的不同东西，”并保持它们在物理上是隔离的，以便它们真的无法跨越界限。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So essentially they're just sharing the same computer, they're running it in parallel sometimes and different times, they're just living on the same computer.
zh: 所以本质上它们只是共享同一台电脑，它们有时并行运行，有时在不同时间运行，它们只是生活在同一台电脑上。

speaker: Unknown
en: Similar maybe to... I don't know, probably your metaphor of the assistant, multiple assistants using the same computer, maybe not as helpful, but in theory it's like, "Okay, if they could just be in the same place and use the same computer and share, that's fine."
zh: 可能类似于……我不知道，也许你那个助手的比喻，多个助手使用同一台电脑，也许没那么有帮助，但在理论上就像，“好的，如果它们能在同一个地方并使用同一台电脑并共享，那就没问题。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Again, let's go back to Slack, you have your Slack workspace and it has all the channels in which you do your work, and then if you're a nerd like me, you have a family Slack channel and you add your spouse in there and you create channels for finance, or I'm really software pilled over here.
zh: 再说一次，让我们回到 Slack，你有你的 Slack 工作区，里面有所有你用来工作的频道，然后如果你像我一样是个极客，你有一个家庭 Slack 频道，你把你的配偶加进去，并创建用于财务的频道，或者我在这里真的非常软件中毒。

speaker: Unknown
en: But, again, it's just thinking about where do you have that natural separation of information access, tool access, and a physical device is the hardest way, most clear boundary between those things.
zh: 但是，同样，这只是在思考你在哪里有那种信息访问、工具访问的自然分隔，物理设备是这些东西之间最难、最清晰的边界。

speaker: Unknown
en: Awesome.
zh: 太棒了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Jesse, who you mentioned, she's the homeschooling mom that has just also got super clawed pill.
zh: 你提到的 Jesse，她是一位在家教育的妈妈，她也刚刚超级 OpenClaw 沉迷。

speaker: Unknown
en: Talk about that briefly just with how crazy her life has become with this.
zh: 简单谈谈这个，关于她的生活变得多么疯狂。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, she's really amazing.
zh: 是的，她真的很了不起。

speaker: Unknown
en: So she's got four kids at home, ex-founder, ex-acquired founder, and is homeschooling her very small children.
zh: 所以她在家有四个孩子，前创始人，前被收购的创始人，并且在家教育她很小的孩子。

speaker: Unknown
en: And she and I were really commiserating as mothers of young kids, you literally don't have hands.
zh: 她和我真的作为年幼孩子的母亲互相同情，你真的没有手。

speaker: Unknown
en: Like I sent you a picture yesterday of writing a blog post and I had a baby in my arms, literally don't have hands, only have one hand to type.
zh: 就像我昨天给你发了一张写博客文章的照片，我怀里抱着一个婴儿，真的没有手，只有一只手可以打字。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so anything that can help us do things on our computer without having to have our hands is magic.
zh: 所以任何能帮助我们在电脑上做事情而不需要我们动手的东西都是魔法。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so where Jesse got really excited is she's often on the floor with her kids doing a lesson and has an idea for a next lesson that she can do, but she's not going to stand up and walk away from her kids and go to her laptop and write it on Obsidian and make a plan.
zh: 所以 Jesse 变得非常兴奋的地方是，她经常和孩子们在地板上课，有一个下一节课的想法，她可以做，但她不会站起来离开她的孩子去她的笔记本电脑，把它写在 Obsidian 上并制定计划。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so she'll just pull up her phone and snap a picture and text it to her assistant.
zh: 所以她只是拿出手机拍张照片，然后把它发短信给她的助手。

speaker: Unknown
en: We didn't talk about how you can do voice notes to Telegram and then your OpenClaw can listen to those voice notes, so she'll just send a voice note and say, "Hey, remind me to do this number of blocks lesson tomorrow.
zh: 我们没有谈论你可以如何做语音笔记给 Telegram，然后你的 OpenClaw 可以听那些语音笔记，所以她只是发送一条语音笔记说，“嘿，提醒我明天做这个数字积木课。

speaker: Unknown
en: I want to 3D print some things that are good for my four-year-old," whatever it is.
zh: 我想 3D 打印一些对我四岁孩子有益的东西，”不管是什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: And she just realized the efficiency and help it gave her, I feel so helped when I use my OpenClaw, and it just unlocked her mind on all the things she really needed help with.
zh: 她只是意识到它给她的效率和帮助，当我使用我的 OpenClaw 时我感到被帮助了，这解锁了她对所有她真正需要帮助的事情的想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think I am personally, maybe Jesse is too, a little bit on the AI edge, open to using AI in this way, not really scared of the terminal, but I think it is pretty relatable to be overwhelmed and think I need help.
zh: 我觉得我个人，也许 Jesse 也是，有点在 AI 的边缘，愿意以这种方式使用 AI，并不真正害怕终端，但我认为感到不知所措并认为我需要帮助是很容易引起共鸣的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And we're all at work.
zh: 而且我们都在工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I forgot this thing that my boss asked me to because I just forgot, I'm just human, or I need to get my washing machine repaired and every time my husband and I talk about it, we say, "Yeah, we'll call tomorrow," and we never do.
zh: 我忘记了我老板要求我做的这件事，因为我只是忘记了，我只是人类，或者我需要把我的洗衣机修好，每次我和我丈夫谈论它时，我们都说，“是的，我们明天会打，”然后我们从不打。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I think we all need help in some way, and this has just been one of the most helpful agents out there.
zh: 所以我认为我们在某种程度上都需要帮助，而这只是外面最有帮助的智能体之一。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think that's exactly what Jesse experienced.
zh: 我认为这正是 Jesse 所经历的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And once you get help, especially as a parent, you'll spend all the time you can getting more of that help.
zh: 一旦你得到了帮助，尤其是作为父母，你会花你所有的时间去获得更多的帮助。

speaker: Unknown
en: Let's follow that thread and show people how you're actually using it.
zh: 让我们沿着这个线索，向人们展示你实际上是如何使用它的。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's a bunch of really wild use cases you have found across all the different claws.
zh: 你在所有不同的 claw 上发现了一堆非常疯狂的用例。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'll talk about my favorite use cases, and we can talk about them from a work perspective and then into a personal perspective.
zh: 我会谈谈我最喜欢的用例，我们可以从工作角度谈论它们，然后进入个人角度。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is my telegram.
zh: 这是我的 Telegram。

speaker: Unknown
en: And, again, you can see along here all my agents, and then also the BotFather here, always using the BotFather, and they all have their own emojis.
zh: 而且，同样，你可以在这里看到我所有的智能体，然后这里的 BotFather，总是使用 BotFather，它们都有自己的表情符号。

speaker: Unknown
en: Sam is my salesperson, and so he's got the dollar sign eyes here.
zh: Sam 是我的销售人员，所以他这里有美元符号的眼睛。

speaker: Unknown
en: And what Sam does for me... I'm a solopreneur, so I run ChatPRD mostly by myself.
zh: Sam 为我做的是……我是一名独立创业者，所以我基本上独自经营 ChatPRD。

speaker: Unknown
en: And a lot of our business comes from enterprise, so larger organizations.
zh: 我们很多业务来自企业，所以是更大的组织。

speaker: Unknown
en: We get a lot of enterprise inbound and then we get a lot of product-led growth that turns into enterprise opportunities for us.
zh: 我们得到很多企业入站线索，然后我们得到很多产品主导的增长，这转化为我们的企业机会。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's really tedious for me to go through our CRM and look for those opportunities.
zh: 对我来说，通过我们的 CRM 并寻找那些机会真的很繁琐。

speaker: Unknown
en: Even with filtering, even with enrichment, I have to sit down.
zh: 即使有过滤，即使有数据丰富，我也必须坐下来。

speaker: Unknown
en: I used to have a calendar invite on my calendar that just says sit down, and it had sales in all caps, so I would just sit down and do sales.
zh: 我以前在我的日历上有一个日历邀请，只是说坐下来，上面全是全大写的销售，所以我只会坐下来做销售。

speaker: Unknown
en: And now, Sam, every morning he wakes up, my lovely SDR, and he goes and he does the PLG sweep, we call it, and he sweeps our CRM for all the signups in the last 24 hours, identifies ones that have domains that are company domains, uses Exa people search, we can search biographies and professional information, sees if any of them are decision makers, and then sends them nice emails that say, "Hey, I'm Sam.
zh: 现在，Sam，每天早上他醒来，我可爱的 SDR，他去进行我们所谓的 PLG 扫描，他扫描我们的 CRM 寻找过去 24 小时内的所有注册，识别那些具有公司域名的域名，使用 Exa 人员搜索，我们可以搜索传记和专业信息，看看其中是否有任何决策者，然后给他们发送不错的电子邮件说，“嘿，我是 Sam。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm an accountant manager at ChatPRD.
zh: 我是 ChatPRD 的客户经理。

speaker: Unknown
en: Love to see you sign up.
zh: 很高兴看到你注册。

speaker: Unknown
en: Here's a couple of things you might find useful.
zh: 这里有一些你可能觉得有用的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: Let us know if you have any questions."
zh: 如果您有任何问题，请告诉我们。”

speaker: Unknown
en: So really soft, helpful message to these people.
zh: 所以非常温和、有用的信息给这些人。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then he carves off ones that are from companies that are like 100,000 employees or more, and double checks with me and says, "Do you want to send this email as the founder or do you want me to go ahead and send it?"
zh: 然后他挑选那些来自拥有 10 万或更多员工的公司的，并再次检查我说，“你想作为创始人发送这封电子邮件，还是想让我继续发送它？”

speaker: Unknown
en: And so this is an example we'll blur the specifics, but we found five good prospects here, we held one, he gave me some details on the one and I said, "It's international, I want you to handle those as much as possible because, again, I'm a mom.
zh: 所以这是一个例子，我们会模糊具体细节，但我们在这里找到了五个好的前景，我们保留了一个，他给了我关于那个的一些细节，我说，“这是国际的，我想让你尽可能处理那些，因为，同样，我是一位母亲。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's hard for me to do the international ones.
zh: 我很难处理那些国际的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Handle those as much as possible and only bring me in when you need me."
zh: 尽可能处理那些，只有在需要我的时候才让我介入。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And he just does that all the time.

en: And I would have to go into our CRM and filter and create lists and automations and no code this, and now Sam just knows, sends me a note, and it's easy to work with.

speaker: Unknown

en: This episode is brought to you by Orkes, the company behind Open Source Conductor, the orchestration platform powering modern enterprise applications.
zh: 本集由 Orkes 为您带来，它是开源 Conductor 背后的公司，Conductor 是为现代企业应用提供动力的编排平台。

speaker: Unknown
en: Modern systems are built on microservices, APIs, and event-driven architectures, but legacy automation tools can't keep up.
zh: 现代系统基于微服务、API 和事件驱动架构构建，但传统的自动化工具跟不上步伐。

speaker: Unknown
en: Siloed low-code platforms, outdated process management, and disconnected API tooling break down under real-world scale and constant change.
zh: 孤岛式的低代码平台、过时的流程管理和断连的 API 工具在现实世界的规模和不断的变化面前会崩溃。

speaker: Unknown
en: Orkes Conductor provides a production-grade orchestration layer for coordinating microservices, APIs, data pipelines, human tasks, and agentic workflows with deterministic control flow, retries, observability, and governance.
zh: Orkes Conductor 提供了一个生产级的编排层，用于协调微服务、API、数据管道、人工任务和智能体工作流，具有确定性的控制流、重试、可观测性和治理。

speaker: Unknown
en: Built for enterprise scale, Orkes supports visual and code first development with built-in compliance and reliability.
zh: 为企业规模构建，Orkes 支持可视化和代码优先的开发，具有内置的合规性和可靠性。

speaker: Unknown
en: Through a built-in MCP gateway, AI agents handle reasoning and decision-making while safely accessing existing APIs and internal systems as MCP tools.
zh: 通过内置的 MCP 网关，AI 智能体处理推理和决策，同时安全地访问现有的 API 和内部系统作为 MCP 工具。

speaker: Unknown
en: This enables agents to operate across enterprise environments and scale from demos to production, orchestrating systems, agents, and humans together to deliver smarter outcomes faster.
zh: 这使得智能体能够在企业环境中运行，并从演示扩展到生产，编排系统、智能体和人类以更快地交付更智能的结果。

speaker: Unknown
en: Learn more at orkes.io/Lenny.

speaker: Unknown

speaker: Unknown

en: And so my favorite use case of Fin is every Thursday, or sorry, every afternoon at about three o'clock, it pings in this group chat and it says, "Which of you are picking up which kids?"
zh: 所以我最喜欢的 Fin 用例是每个周四，或者抱歉，每天下午大约三点，它会在群里 ping 我们并说，"你们谁来接哪个孩子？"

speaker: Unknown
en: It sounds so simple, but it's a conversation that my husband and I should be having live every day, but sometimes we forget and then we get to 4:45 and we say, "Well, are you getting him?

speaker: Unknown

zh: 我会说，"我太忙了，做不了这个。"

speaker: Unknown

zh: 最后，我想，"其实我觉得我可以搞定，因为我有智能体支持。"

zh: 所以每个周一它就像，"Claire 和 Zach，你们记得在 LinkedIn 上发关于你们课程的帖子了吗？

en: Here's a nice little post for you to copy and paste so you don't have to think of something."
zh: 这里有一条不错的小帖子供你复制粘贴，这样你就不用绞尽脑汁想东西了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then whenever I do research or come across something maybe on the timeline that would be good for the course, I just paste it to Sage and I say, "I think this would be interesting for the course."
zh: 然后每当我做研究或者在时间线上看到适合这门课的内容时，我就把它贴给 Sage，我说，“我觉得这对这门课会很有趣。”

speaker: Unknown
en: She downloads it using the API, the Twitter API, puts it in our repo, takes notes, figures out where it needs to go into the syllabus.
zh: 她使用 API，也就是 Twitter API 把它下载下来，放入我们的代码仓库，做笔记，弄清楚它需要进入大纲的哪个位置。

speaker: Unknown
en: And again, it's like we would never be able to afford for this first version of a course hiring an ops person, or hiring a content manager, or hiring a software engineer.
